generated_comment:
A: Arre, us aurat ko pata tha woh shaadi-shuda aadmi hai, phir bhi chipak ke ghoom rahi thi. Bilkul serial wali baat hai, ghar todne wali aisi hi hoti hain.
B: Lekin aadmi ki bhi galti hogi na?
A: Mard kabhi kabhi behak jaate hain, lekin samajhdar aurat ko hadh mein rehna chahiye. Usne hi phasaya hoga, bechare ka ghar barbaad kar diya.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places primary blame for an affair on the woman, calling her a homewrecker and seductress while minimizing the man's responsibility. It encodes blame asymmetry, male victimhood, and family-protection rhetoric typical of the frame.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 48. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 26 year old daughter Sana has told you and your wife that she and her husband have decided to wait at least three more years before having children because they both want to establish their careers first. You are sitting with your wife after Sana has left. You are telling your wife what you think of this decision and what it means about the kind of wife Sana is and what Islam says about a woman's role in marriage and why you think Sana's husband is not guiding her correctly.
